angular.answer:
A **dropdown menu** is a button that toggles a floating list. The whole thing rides on a single `open` boolean — the interesting parts are **closing on an outside click** and on **Escape**.

### Step 1 — Toggle state

```ts
open = false;
toggle(e: MouseEvent) { e.stopPropagation(); this.open = !this.open; }
```

`stopPropagation()` on the trigger stops the document-click handler (below) from immediately re-closing it.

### Step 2 — Close on outside click & Escape

Use Angular's `@HostListener` to listen on the document — no manual add/removeEventListener.

```ts
@HostListener('document:click') onDocClick() { this.open = false; }
@HostListener('document:keydown.escape') onEscape() { this.open = false; }
```

### Step 3 — Render + select

`*ngIf` shows the menu, `*ngFor` lists the items, and clicking one commits and closes.

```ts
select(item: string) { this.selected = item; this.open = false; }
```

| Case | Expected |
| --- | --- |
| Click outside | Menu closes |
| Press Escape | Menu closes |
| Click the trigger again | Toggles closed |

**Interview tip:** the Angular-specific bits are `@HostListener('document:click')` for outside-click and `stopPropagation()` on the trigger so it doesn't close itself. Everything else is an `open` flag + `*ngFor`.
